Dev/vscode.title: vscode
Dev/vscode.content:
Use VS Code for Latex

Download and install Tex Live

Install it from tug.org/texlive/windows.html by clicking install-tl-windows.exe.
Double-click the file once downloaded, and click install to start
The default options are mostly good enough.


Download and install VS Code
4. You can download it from code.visualstudio.com/. The installation is easy.
Install extension LaTex Workshop in VS Code

Open VS Code
Click the Extensions button in the left sidebar (see below)
Find the LaTex Workshop extension and click Install. (There might be a view different ones. Make sure to install the one by James Yu)



Write and Compile
4. File→Open folder (choose one folder of latex files)
5. Click the first button in the left sidebar for viewing files in the folder. Click a .tex file there to open it.
6. In the top right corner of the tex file (see below), there are two buttons (the triangle one for compiling and the one next for viewing pdf).
7. In the left panel, there is a Tex button. It contains many other options to play with.

Forward synctex: shortcut ctrl+alt+j will match the cursor location  in the tex file to the pdf file. Or you could add an option in the context menu when clicking the right mouse button:

Click extension button in the left side bar and click the extension LaTex Workshop.
There is a small setting button next to the Uninstall button (as in the above figure). Click it to open the settings
There is a setting “Show Context Menu” there. (The list of settings is long. You could search “context” to find it.)
Click to enable it.
Back to a tex file and click the right mouse button. There should be a “SyncTex from Cursor” there.


Backward synctex: the default shortcut ctrl+click will match the cursor location in the pdf file to the tex file. You could change it to double-click in Latex Workshop settings “SyncTex:keybinding”.

Use Github Copilot for autocomplete

Register a Github education account from github.com/education (It might take a few days for github to approve it.)
Install two extensions Github Copilot and Github Copilot Chat in VS Code (similar to the installation of LaTex Workshop)
In the settings of Github Copilot, enable the option “Enable Auto Completions”
When you typing in VS Code, some codes will be generated automatically. Use the “Tab” key to accept the generated codes.
For example, when you tying a comment in latex like ”% add definition of convex functions” and typing “\begin{definition}”, some codes will be generated automatically.
